brief_justification: Correct answer (Yes) with valid Bayesian reasoning; Multi-level analysis tracing both causal pathways and applying Bayes' theorem with the unobserved confounder consideration; Some self-monitoring via labeled suppression/amplification signals and assumption audit but no spontaneous course-correction; Basic verification by checking the confounder doesn't change the conclusion, but no counterfactual or boundary checks; Acknowledges the confounder role and notes the reasoning holds regardless, but doesn't quantify uncertainty; Considers the confounder pathway as a potential alternative explanation but doesn't deeply evaluate competing hypotheses; Full derivation trail from DAG to Bayes' theorem to conclusion, reproducible by a third party
